codeReview.summary: The responsive CSS work added 375px/424px breakpoints and broad 44px mobile min-heights, but two contract requirements are not met at 375px: slippage typography is reduced below 0.75rem and slippage preset width drops below 44px. The commit also includes non-CSS feature code (wei/details + token error formatting), which is out of scope for this feature review.

issues.description: At the 375px breakpoint, slippage typography is explicitly reduced to 0.6875rem (`.slippage-preset-compact`, `.slippage-box-input`, `.slippage-box-label`, `.slippage-box-hint` at lines 2237, 2242, 2245, 2248). This violates the feature/contract requirement that slippage labels and presets be >= 0.75rem (VAL-CSS-001).
At the 375px breakpoint, `.slippage-preset-compact` is set to `min-width: 40px`, which drops below the required 44px mobile touch target (VAL-CSS-005).

The css-polish commit includes unrelated behavioral changes (wei amount rendering and token-ref error formatting/copy handlers around lines 5272-5614). This scope bleed makes per-feature traceability and review harder.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker verified computed styles but still marked the feature as passing despite measurements that contradict the contract thresholds. The skill/procedure should force explicit pass/fail checks against each numeric requirement at the smallest required viewport.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows `slippage preset` at 375px as `box: 40x44` and `font: 11px`, while the handoff states VAL-CSS-001..005 were fulfilled.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature isolation/commit hygiene appears weak: a prior feature handoff had `commitId: pending`, and this css-polish commit includes non-css implementation work. Consider documenting a strict rule to commit only scoped changes with a clean working tree before ending a feature run.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `wei-and-address-display` handoff has `commitId: pending`; `git show 5e23b81` includes both CSS breakpoint edits and JS/detail rendering changes unrelated to css-polish-responsive.

summary: Review failed: css-polish-responsive does not fully satisfy VAL-CSS-001 and VAL-CSS-005 at 375px due to 11px slippage text and 40px slippage preset width. Report written with two blocking issues and one non-blocking scope-isolation note.
